title: Fast Local Feedback, Hooks, and Developer Experience · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Create fast local gates that match CI without blocking ordinary iteration. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a one-command local verification workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Expose stable format, lint, type, test, and verify commands independent of tool internals.
Run fast deterministic changed-file checks in local hooks and keep heavier checks elsewhere.
Treat local hooks as convenience while CI remains authoritative and bypasses stay visible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Command Interface · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates local hooks. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Expose stable format, lint, type, test, and verify commands independent of tool internals. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.text: Developers should memorize every underlying command. This fails the Command Interface contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Feedback Quality case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates local hooks. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A nonzero exit code is sufficient developer feedback.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make failures concise, reproducible locally, and linked to remediation. Artifact under review: a one-command local verification workflow.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feedback Quality, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Feedback Quality applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make failures concise, reproducible locally, and linked to remediation.
